back_translation: And it shall be, if listening you will listen to the voice of the Lord your God, to keep to do all His commandments that I am commanding you today, then the Lord your God will set you high above all the nations of the earth.
And all these blessings will come upon you and overtake you, for you will listen to the voice of the Lord your God.
Blessed are you in the city, and blessed are you in the field.
Blessed is the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground and the fruit of your livestock, the calves of your herds and the lambs of your flocks.
Blessed is your basket and your kneading bowl.
Blessed are you in your coming in, and blessed are you in your going out.
The Lord will give your enemies who rise against you to be defeated before you; in one way they will come out against you, and in seven ways they will flee before you.
The Lord will command with you the blessing in your barns and in all that your hand sends forth, and He will bless you in the land that the Lord your God is giving to you.
The Lord will establish you to Himself as a holy people, as He has sworn to you, when you keep the commandments of the Lord your God and walk in His ways.
And all the peoples of the earth shall see that the name of the Lord is called upon you, and they shall fear from you.

And the Lord will make you have abundance for good, in the fruit of your womb and in the fruit of your livestock and in the fruit of your ground, upon the ground that the Lord swore to your fathers to give to you.

The Lord will open for you His good storehouse, the heavens, to give the rain of your land in its time, and to bless every work of your hand; and you shall lend to many nations, and you shall not borrow.

And the Lord will make you the head and not the tail, and you shall be only upward and not be downward, when you listen to the commandments of the Lord your God that I am commanding you today, to keep and to do.
And you shall not turn aside from all the words that I am commanding you today, right or left, to walk after other gods to serve them.

And it shall be, if you do not listen to the voice of the Lord your God, to keep to do all His commandments and His statutes that I am commanding you today, then all these curses will come upon you and overtake you.
Cursed are you in the city, and cursed are you in the field.
Cursed is your basket and your kneading bowl.
Cursed is the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground, the calves of your herds and the lambs of your flocks.
Cursed are you in your coming in, and cursed are you in your going out.

The Lord will send upon you the curse, the confusion, and the rebuke in all that your hand does that you will do, until you are destroyed and quickly perish, because of the evil of your deeds that you have forsaken Me.
The Lord will make the plague cling to you, until He has finished you from upon the ground where you are going to possess it.
The Lord will strike you with consumption and with fever and with inflammation and with scorching heat and with the sword and with blight and with mildew, and they shall pursue you until you perish.
And your heavens that are over your head shall be bronze, and the earth that is under you shall be iron.
The Lord will give the rain of your land dust and powder; from the heavens it shall come down upon you until you are destroyed.

The Lord will give you defeated before your enemies; in one way you shall come out against him, and in seven ways you shall flee before him, and you shall become an object of horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.
And your corpse shall be food for every bird of the heavens and for the beast of the earth, and there will be no one to frighten them.
The Lord will strike you with the boils of Egypt and with the tumors and with the scab and with the itch, from which you cannot be healed.
The Lord will strike you with madness and with blindness and with confusion of heart.

And you shall grope about at noon as the blind man gropes in the darkness, and you shall not prosper in your ways, and you shall be only oppressed and plundered all the days, and there shall be no one to save.
A woman you shall betroth, and another man shall lie with her; a house you shall build, and you shall not dwell in it; a vineyard you shall plant, and you shall not begin to use it.

Your ox shall be slaughtered before your eyes, and you shall not eat of it; your donkey shall be taken away from before you, and shall not return to you; your flock shall be given to your enemies, and there shall be no savior for you.
Your sons and your daughters shall be given to another people, and your eyes shall look and grow weary toward them all the day, and there shall be no power in your hand.
The fruit of your ground and all your toil a people whom you do not know shall eat, and you shall be only oppressed and crushed all the days.
And you shall be driven mad by the sight of your eyes that you shall see.
The Lord will strike you with an evil boil upon the knees and upon the thighs, from which you cannot be healed, from the sole of your foot and unto the top of your head.
The Lord will lead you and your king whom you set over you to a nation that you have not known, you and your fathers, and there you shall serve other gods, wood and stone.
And you shall become an object of horror, a proverb, and a byword, among all the peoples where the Lord will drive you there.
Much seed you shall bring out to the field, and little you shall gather, for the locust shall consume it.
Vineyards you shall plant and tend, but wine you shall not drink and not gather, for the worm shall eat it.
Olive trees you shall have in all your territory, but with oil you shall not anoint yourself, for your olive shall drop.
Sons and daughters you shall father, but they shall not be yours, for they shall go into captivity.
All your trees and the fruit of your ground the swarming locust shall possess.
The foreigner who is in your midst shall ascend upon you higher and higher, and you shall come down lower and lower.
He shall lend to you, and you shall not lend to him; he shall be the head, and you shall be the tail.

And all these curses shall come upon you and pursue you and overtake you until you are destroyed, for you did not listen to the voice of the Lord your God to keep His commandments and His statutes that He commanded you.
And they shall be in you for a sign and for a wonder, and in your seed forever.
Because you did not serve the Lord your God with joy and with good of heart, out of the abundance of all things.

And you shall serve your enemies whom the Lord will send against you, in famine and in thirst and in nakedness and in lack of everything; and He shall put a yoke of iron on your neck until He has destroyed you.
The Lord will lift up against you a nation from afar, from the end of the earth, as the eagle swoops down, a nation whose language you shall not understand,
a nation hard of face, that shall not respect the old and the young it shall not pity.

And he shall eat the fruit of your livestock and the fruit of your ground until you are destroyed, who shall not leave for you grain, new wine and oil, the calves of your herds and the lambs of your flocks, until he has caused you to perish.

And he shall besiege you in all your gates, until your high and fortified walls come down in which you trust in all your land; and he shall besiege you in all your gates in all your land that the Lord your God has given to you.
And you shall eat the fruit of your womb, the flesh of your sons and your daughters whom the Lord your God has given to you, in the siege and in the distress that your enemy will inflict on you.
The most gentle and refined man among you, his eye shall be evil toward his brother and toward the wife of his bosom and toward the rest of his sons whom he shall leave,
from giving to any of them from the flesh of his sons that he shall eat, because nothing is left to him in the siege and in the distress that your enemy shall inflict on you in all your gates.

The gentle and refined woman among you, who did not try the sole of her foot to set on the ground from delicacy and from tenderness, her eye shall be evil toward the man of her bosom and toward her son and toward her daughter,

and toward her afterbirth that comes out from between her legs, and toward her sons whom she bears, for she shall eat them in lack of everything in secret, in the siege and in the distress that your enemy shall inflict on you in your gates.
If you do not keep to do all the words of this law that are written in this book, to fear this glorious and awesome name, the Lord your God,
then the Lord will make extraordinary your strokes and the strokes of your seed, great and lasting strokes and evil and lasting sicknesses.
And He will bring back upon you all the diseases of Egypt that you dreaded from before them, and they shall cling to you.
Also every sickness and every stroke that is not written in the book of this law, the Lord shall bring them upon you until you are destroyed.
And you shall be left few in number, instead of having been like the stars of the heavens in multitude, because you did not listen to the voice of the Lord your God.

And it shall be, just as the Lord rejoiced over you to do good to you and to multiply you, so the Lord shall rejoice over you to cause you to perish and to destroy you, and you shall be torn from upon the ground where you are going there to possess it.

And the Lord shall scatter you among all the peoples, from one end of the earth and unto the other end of the earth, and there you shall serve other gods that you have not known, you and your fathers, wood and stone.

And among those nations you shall not find rest, and there shall not be a resting place for the sole of your foot, and the Lord shall give you there a trembling heart and failing of eyes and despairing of soul.
And your life shall be hanging for you in front; and you shall fear night and day, and you shall not trust in your life.

In the morning you shall say: Who will give evening? And in the evening you shall say: Who will give morning?—from the fear of your heart that you shall fear, and from the sight of your eyes that you shall see.

And the Lord shall bring you back to Egypt in ships, by the way that I said to you you shall not see it again, and you shall sell yourselves there to your enemies as male slaves and as female slaves, and there shall be no buyer.
These are the words of the covenant that the Lord commanded Moses to cut with the sons of Israel in the land of Moab, besides the covenant that He cut with them at Horeb.
